chunks.text: When I arrived at the station, my friend had already departed on his train.
I caught my breath and sat down on the bench, feeling exhausted.
Actually, I had left home late.
The road had been under repair since previously, and I had not known the detour.
Meanwhile my friend had reached the station on time.
The staff said he had approached the train only a few minutes before.
I had not been able to recognize his face in the crowd.
I felt sorry, but there was nothing I could do.
I had to wait for the next train.
He would get off at the next station and come back for me.
I realize now that he has always held this gentle kindness.
On a small bench in front of the station, I remained there, waiting for him.
The clouds vanished, and eventually the sky grew bright.
